rows.source_raw: Millennium Empire - Léxandro's dream of an empire to last throughout the Eras. He knew well how the Arcanists, despite their greatness, could not last past their prime. Nor could the old monarchies of the Divine Era. Even the Beasts of Jor did not stand the test of time. It is the FASCISTS who will create lasting order. Only them.

rows.source_raw: To do so, either double click - or hold shift/the left trigger.
rows.context_en: To do so, either double click - or hold shift/the left trigger.

rows.source_raw: Varjomieli - Mind of Shadows. A type of lichdom created through primal stasis, usually by powerful druids or elven wizards. Unlike ordinary liches, they do not inspire as much dread nor hold as much power, due to their balance with the natural world. In other words, they do not consume souls to keep living, but rather just stay permanent.

Unlike ordinary liches, they do not inspire as much dread nor hold as much power, due to their balance with the natural world. In other words, they do not consume souls to keep living, but rather just stay permanent.

rows.source_raw: Coast War - the great war. A conflict between the Arcanists of Reeds, as well as every other nation connected with them across the Coast. A horrible waste of human lives, which claimed millions of lives. There was no honor in this fighting. That you know.
rows.context_en: Coast War - the great war. A conflict between the Arcanists of Reeds, as well as every other nation connected with them across the Coast. A horrible waste of human lives, which claimed millions of lives. There was no honor in this fighting. That you know.

rows.source_raw: Responsibility. That is the weight she feels weighing upon her shoulders. Modissa does not see herself, her EGO, as having any greater meaning. Rather, that meaning has been transposed from others. Her mentor. Her friends. But most importantly, her ideology.
rows.context_en: Responsibility. That is the weight she feels weighing upon her shoulders. Modissa does not see herself, her EGO, as having any greater meaning. Rather, that meaning has been transposed from others. Her mentor. Her friends. But most importantly, her ideology.

rows.source_raw: Almost Lichdom - Inspired by your favorite pale man, you no longer gain health from healing. You also become invulnerable to all forms of damage. Whenever you take damage of any kind, you always gain one level of exhaustion. Whenever you heal more than 6 HP from any one source, you lose one level of exhaustion.
rows.context_en: Almost Lichdom - Inspired by your favorite pale man, you no longer gain health from healing. You also become invulnerable to all forms of damage. Whenever you take damage of any kind, you always gain one level of exhaustion. Whenever you heal more than 6 HP from any one source, you lose one level of exhaustion.

rows.source_raw: Fortress of Verdure - a perfectly square miles-wide urban expanse placed at the center of the Verdure Hills. Nothing grows around it, and there are no entrances. One of the great mysteries of Askan, said to be the cursed city of some forgotten magocracy. Those who have attempted to scale its wall and survived say its filled with undeath and general negative energy.

One of the great mysteries of Askan, said to be the cursed city of some forgotten magocracy. Those who have attempted to scale its wall and survived say its filled with undeath and general negative energy.

rows.source_raw: Norvikian Crown - the official currency of Norvik. Suffered heavy inflation during the years after Urth's Passing. A meal is somewhere between five to twenty crowns. A cheap month's rent is a hundred crowns. A spellbook is fifty crowns. An average salary outside the city is up to three hundred. Average salary inside the city is up to five hundred.
rows.context_en: Norvikian Crown - the official currency of Norvik. Suffered heavy inflation during the years after Urth's Passing. A meal is somewhere between five to twenty crowns. A cheap month's rent is a hundred crowns. A spellbook is fifty crowns. An average salary outside the city is up to three hundred. Average salary inside the city is up to five hundred.

rows.source_raw: Proximity Rule - a rule of alignment. If an action is taken for the benefit of a target, then if said target is closer emotionally to you, it will result in a less good action. If a target is further away from you, such as a stranger, performing selfless actions for them will result in more goodness.
rows.context_en: Proximity Rule - a rule of alignment. If an action is taken for the benefit of a target, then if said target is closer emotionally to you, it will result in a less good action. If a target is further away from you, such as a stranger, performing selfless actions for them will result in more goodness.

rows.source_raw: Robelight Night - before the eternal darkness and intense cold of Askanii winter begins, a group of women are selected in Norvik to perform a series of chants on the banks of River Torna. It is said that in the past, these women would be allowed to walk upon the water and cross the river through their love of Urth.

rows.source_raw: Ah. Of course. It is not you she hates. She hates herself. The human part of herself. The part which places her below her glorious golden celestial masters. If she could sever that humanity, she would do so in an instant, all for a slim chance to hear the choir.

rows.source_raw: In the halls of power, the arcane shall be our shield and our sword. Yet, remember this: the arcanist's duty is not only to himself but to the state and its people. For in the endless pursuit of knowledge, one must not forget the price of ignorance. A vigilant mind is the guardian of civilization, and a vigilant state the fortress of progress. Let no shadow fall upon our vigilance, for in the light of our knowledge lies the path to true dominion.

For in the endless pursuit of knowledge, one must not forget the price of ignorance. A vigilant mind is the guardian of civilization, and a vigilant state the fortress of progress. Let no shadow fall upon our vigilance, for in the light of our knowledge lies the path to true dominion.

rows.source_raw: Equestrian - Urthguard members trained in horsemanship. Sometimes referred to as the Knights of Norvik. They are few in numbers, but function as elite soldiers, usually dealing in politics in the Palace, or sent to deal with high-level situations. They also do work as foreign delegates, and most of them are nobles, for entirely honorable reasons.

They also do work as foreign delegates, and most of them are nobles, for entirely honorable reasons.

rows.source_raw: An insane demi-lich. His mind having shattered many decades ago. However, his behavior (along with the simply fact that he exists down here in a relatively stable condition) suggests his madness has either not entirely set in, or that he is being shepherded. Perhaps by the pale man upstairs?

Perhaps by the pale man upstairs?

rows.source_raw: Hae'Xi - one of eight bands along the Coast. Means either 'Growing Maw' or 'Into the Air' in abyssal. A deeply cursed jungle hellscape filled with volcanic rivers, flesheating insects, and murderous plants. Once an elven sanctuary, now home to demonic warlords, tieflings cities, and celestial crusaders.

rows.source_raw: Shadow Lord - an early walking god of the Divine Era. Known for his legion of Death Knights, and the grasp they held over Old Diminor for close to a century. His city, Savalni, still stands today, and is where the remaining Shadow Clerics gather.

If the old guard Nationalists do not represent them- if Freestriderism does not support them- if Azgalism is way too... progressive- then what frays will these rumblings lead us along?

rows.source_raw: Kargamesh is on the other side of the world. It's an opposite to Askani - a mirror. In their dry we have wet. In their warm we have cold. She'll gladly tell you all about it- on a surface level. She's not here to make friends, not really. Or at least not yet.

rows.source_raw: If you ever feel discontent about his behavior, remember: this is a person. He is not a representative of his entire people. Just as there are humans who are rude and boisterous and egotistical, there are owlfolk of the same cloth. All folk are different. All folk are the same. This figure is a bit eccentric. Do not let that darken your vision of all other bewinged brother and sisters across the Coast.

rows.source_raw: Rule Pockets - an Esoteric Pocket category where one rule of reality, magic, or otherwise is changed. Sometimes in subtle ways, such as slightly lowering gravity or speeding up time. And sometimes in major ways, such as death being impossible, or all liquids becoming flammable. Yes, including your own blood.

rows.source_raw: Green Troll - standing only slightly taller than the average human, these trolls live among the moss and stone of Askanii woods. They are hunter-gatherers, and seldom organize into anything resembling civilization. Though they mostly avoid humans since the Conquest, they are known to both attack travellers and raid settlements near their territory.

Though they mostly avoid humans since the Conquest, they are known to both attack travellers and raid settlements near their territory.

rows.source_raw: Borgo Darrow - the first paladin of Urth. Founded the Urthguard, as well as the illustrious Darrow bloodline. Known for his bravery, and inability to be damaged in battle. Died of heart attack at age 40. His passing is used as an example of the importance of aging men to keep track of cardiovascular diseases.

rows.source_raw: The Workers' Party leadership speaks of "patience" and "gradual progress" while our people suffer. They celebrate universal suffrage while nine out of ten guild leaders remain human men. They praise our "egalitarian" city while women earn less across every sector, while Othered folktypes are denied citizenship for their so-called "monstrous" traits, while halflings face deportation to the Horde as every major faction turns away.

They praise our "egalitarian" city while women earn less across every sector, while Othered folktypes are denied citizenship for their so-called "monstrous" traits, while halflings face deportation to the Horde as every major faction turns away.

rows.source_raw: Water Temple - a half-frozen, half-submerged spiral maze in the City Below. Found underneath the mouth of the River Torna and is said to suck in a portion of the river's water, sending it to the Undersea. A similar temple is said to be found at the start of the river, far inland, near the edges of the Infinite Wastes.

rows.source_raw: Greater Restoration - a fifth level Abjuration spell. It undoes one debilitating effect from a target. This can include things like petrification, long-term curses, or most types of major ailments or diseases. While harder to cast these days, it is still commonly employed by churches and places of healing across the Coast.
rows.context_en: Greater Restoration - a fifth level Abjuration spell. It undoes one debilitating effect from a target. This can include things like petrification, long-term curses, or most types of major ailments or diseases. While harder to cast these days, it is still commonly employed by churches and places of healing across the Coast.

rows.source_raw: Patriarch Pilli - the second Lord Patriarch of Norvik. Appointed by Urth after the death of the First Patriarch. Pilli is known for his organizational nature and attitude to feverishly writing down and retaining Norvikian culture, and the Order of Urth.
rows.context_en: Patriarch Pilli - the second Lord Patriarch of Norvik. Appointed by Urth after the death of the First Patriarch. Pilli is known for his organizational nature and attitude to feverishly writing down and retaining Norvikian culture, and the Order of Urth.

rows.source_raw: Battle of Thazen - one of many great battles you read about in military history class. It is said to be the beginning of the end of the Coast War, due to the new methods of anti-esoteric war that caused great destruction for all seven sides of the conflict. It ended after three weeks of continual slaughter, with the fall of Thazen. It ended on 14th of Chronus, 481 AE

rows.source_raw: The more you stare into those deranged eyes of his, the more you understand- he is not full of hatred from some idealistic vision of the future. He does not view the future of Askanii as a goblin paradise. All he wants - entirely due to some personal vendetta fueled by sociopathy - is to erase humans from existence. Gulp.

rows.source_raw: As Snurre attempted to copy the foreword, the esoterically summoned spirit of Cividix himself became near corporeal. But you handled it. You got a bit possessed, but you handled it, allowing him to both copy the foreword and gain great insight into the secrets of Cividix.

rows.source_raw: Azgalist - Whenever you take damage, move one step forward on a secret three step counter. On step one, a 'thesis' d20 die is thrown. On step two, if the damage you took is higher than the 'thesis' die, a 'antithesis' d4 die is thrown. On step three, the damage you took is lowered either by 1, or by the 'antithesis' die if you rolled one during step two. This is 'synthesis', somehow. The next time you take damage, return to step one.

On step three, the damage you took is lowered either by 1, or by the 'antithesis' die if you rolled one during step two. This is 'synthesis', somehow. The next time you take damage, return to step one.

rows.source_raw: Prestidigitation - a very convenient, but difficult to master, transmutation cantrip. It can do pretty much anything, from heating tea or cleaning clothes, to creating minor sensory effects or temporary objects. It's extremely versatile, and a talented user is able to wield it into even greater forms of usefulness.

rows.source_raw: Detect Secret - a rare 1st level divination spell. It allows the user to notice illusions, traps, and other hidden and generally secret things within a certain range. What exactly constitutes a 'secret' is under debate, but it appears to differ on the caster's own interpretation of this word. The spell was created late in the Arcane Era by copying the powers from a Wand of Secrets.
rows.context_en: Detect Secret - a rare 1st level divination spell. It allows the user to notice illusions, traps, and other hidden and generally secret things within a certain range. What exactly constitutes a 'secret' is under debate, but it appears to differ on the caster's own interpretation of this word. The spell was created late in the Arcane Era by copying the powers from a Wand of Secrets.

rows.source_raw: The more advanced governmental structures of both federated magedoms, parliamentary republics, and constitutional empires all grow partly due to their bourgeoisifying nature, and modern city-state industrial developments.
rows.context_en: The more advanced governmental structures of both federated magedoms, parliamentary republics, and constitutional empires all grow partly due to their bourgeoisifying nature, and modern city-state industrial developments.

rows.source_raw: Voice of God - Whenever you roll a d20, another hidden d20 is rolled. If that die is the same as your die, you critically succeed. If both are natural twenties, you regain all your hit points, you regain all your spent spell slots, you lose all levels of exhaustion, and you become genuinely happy for 1d4 seconds.
rows.context_en: Voice of God - Whenever you roll a d20, another hidden d20 is rolled. If that die is the same as your die, you critically succeed. If both are natural twenties, you regain all your hit points, you regain all your spent spell slots, you lose all levels of exhaustion, and you become genuinely happy for 1d4 seconds.

rows.source_raw: We cannot ignore legitimate concerns about economic inequality and the concentration of wealth. When guild membership fees burden working families, when our prosperity flows eastward instead of benefiting those who created it, we must act - but we must act wisely.
rows.context_en: We cannot ignore legitimate concerns about economic inequality and the concentration of wealth. When guild membership fees burden working families, when our prosperity flows eastward instead of benefiting those who created it, we must act - but we must act wisely.

rows.source_raw: Order of the First Knights - when you picture a knight, a holy warriors acting as the moral cornerstone of any number of civilizations in Old Diminor, this is where it all began. The First Knights sadly disbanded during the early Coast War, but their ideals still live on to this day. Even your Urthguard armor is directly based on their famous designs.

The First Knights sadly disbanded during the early Coast War, but their ideals still live on to this day. Even your Urthguard armor is directly based on their famous designs.

rows.source_raw: Historical materialism - a form of thought arguing that societies throughout time develop based on the way people produce and distribute goods, with these "material conditions" creating social classes with conflicting interests that drive historical change through inevitable struggles for power and resources.
rows.context_en: Historical materialism - a form of thought arguing that societies throughout time develop based on the way people produce and distribute goods, with these "material conditions" creating social classes with conflicting interests that drive historical change through inevitable struggles for power and resources.

rows.source_raw: Communing Patron - If you take damage that would cause you to go to 0, a mysterious force protects you. If this happens, you take no damage, and lose one 1st level spellslot. If you do not have any 1st level spell slots, this does not happen.
rows.context_en: Communing Patron - If you take damage that would cause you to go to 0, a mysterious force protects you. If this happens, you take no damage, and lose one 1st level spellslot. If you do not have any 1st level spell slots, this does not happen.

rows.source_raw: The Coast - also known as Jor's Realm. It is a strip of land divided into eight bands. The further inland you go, the closer you get to the infinite wastes. It is theorized to be a ring or a half-sphere due to the bend of the horizon.
rows.context_en: The Coast - also known as Jor's Realm. It is a strip of land divided into eight bands. The further inland you go, the closer you get to the infinite wastes. It is theorized to be a ring or a half-sphere due to the bend of the horizon.

rows.source_raw: Troll - a monster native to Askan. They exist in broad categories, from small and mostly harmless green trolls to mutated volatile curse trolls. They vary in intelligence, strength, and organizational abilities, but they are a core enemy of the Urthfolk. It was Urth who destroyed their rising kingdom during the late Arcane Era.
rows.context_en: Troll - a monster native to Askan. They exist in broad categories, from small and mostly harmless green trolls to mutated volatile curse trolls. They vary in intelligence, strength, and organizational abilities, but they are a core enemy of the Urthfolk. It was Urth who destroyed their rising kingdom during the late Arcane Era.

rows.source_raw: Vulgata - a lost religious text originally passed down within the ranks of the Order of the First Knights in Old Diminor. These great warriors used the teachings in this secretive and holy scripture to ultimately influence all modern ideas of chivarly and knighthood. All copies were said to have been destroyed when the order disbanded.
rows.context_en: Vulgata - a lost religious text originally passed down within the ranks of the Order of the First Knights in Old Diminor. These great warriors used the teachings in this secretive and holy scripture to ultimately influence all modern ideas of chivarly and knighthood. All copies were said to have been destroyed when the order disbanded.

rows.source_raw: Ishkii - one of the three goblin tribes in Norvik. Commonly called the Water Goblins, due to their river expertise. Led by a council of three Nilbogs, housed in a lighthouse (though they call it a wizard's tower) in Dvârgby.
rows.context_en: Ishkii - one of the three goblin tribes in Norvik. Commonly called the Water Goblins, due to their river expertise. Led by a council of three Nilbogs, housed in a lighthouse (though they call it a wizard's tower) in Dvârgby.

rows.source_raw: Known as the greatest human warriors of the Fade Era, these humans were known to inhabit both Divinii Torum and parts of northern Askanii during their service to the red dragon Esuth. Also known as Men of Fire and River, although the book laments the lost spelling of their original name, which seems to originate in the same tongue as the owl-folk of Divinii Torum.

Also known as Men of Fire and River, although the book laments the lost spelling of their original name, which seems to originate in the same tongue as the owl-folk of Divinii Torum.

rows.source_raw: Recent events have shown us the challenges our city faces. The protests at the Towers, ongoing disputes with various guilds, and concerns about the Golden Line policies have highlighted the gap between those who have ruled Norvik after Urth's fading, and Norvik's working families.
rows.context_en: Recent events have shown us the challenges our city faces. The protests at the Towers, ongoing disputes with various guilds, and concerns about the Golden Line policies have highlighted the gap between those who have ruled Norvik after Urth's fading, and Norvik's working families.

rows.source_raw: Thazen - the greatest of the floating cities. Center of the Haagin-Dim Coalition and captured essence of the nameless Fourth Apprentice of Jor. It was the most esoterically powerful city state in history, and famous for its shadowy thousand feet long tentacles that could be seen in the skies for a hundred miles away. It fell in the year 481 AE. It was rebuilt, now on the ground, in the decades after.

It fell in the year 481 AE. It was rebuilt, now on the ground, in the decades after.

rows.source_raw: Look at what's happening with the guilds. With a divided work force, unionization is nearly impossible. Just ten years ago it was bad enough between dwarf and human conflicts, but these days the Azgal Unions are unable to establish simply because they cannot reach out to the diverse work force.

rows.source_raw: (Yell and point.) "My LORD! An ABERRATION!
rows.context_en: (Yell and point.) "My LORD! An ABERRATION!

rows.source_raw: You're nothing like him. You did not come from nobility. You were not given any special treatment, even with your latent talents. The only thing you have in common is your willingness to throw it all away - to throw yourselves against the meat grinder of TIME. But you will not be minced. Your sinewy nature will grind it to a halt. The farm boy will live past Ragnarôk. The hero's bones will break upon the pavement.
rows.context_en: You're nothing like him. You did not come from nobility. You were not given any special treatment, even with your latent talents. The only thing you have in common is your willingness to throw it all away - to throw yourselves against the meat grinder of TIME. But you will not be minced. Your sinewy nature will grind it to a halt. The farm boy will live past Ragnarôk. The hero's bones will break upon the pavement.

rows.source_raw: Lord Patriarch - the commander of city forces and top cleric beneath Urth. The title is appointed by Urth. Currently Lord Âkesam holds the title, having led the city for the past thirty years. He is a withdrawn leader, over eighty years of age, and has put most power in the hands of the Magistrates.
rows.context_en: Lord Patriarch - the commander of city forces and top cleric beneath Urth. The title is appointed by Urth. Currently Lord Âkesam holds the title, having led the city for the past thirty years. He is a withdrawn leader, over eighty years of age, and has put most power in the hands of the Magistrates.

rows.source_raw: Power Word Ebb - one of the lost Power Words. There are theories on its existence among scholars, mostly focusing on the dragon's search to surpass Jor. The only known mention in the journals of Tisereldanour of the word indicated it had an anti-magic effect.
rows.context_en: Power Word Ebb - one of the lost Power Words. There are theories on its existence among scholars, mostly focusing on the dragon's search to surpass Jor. The only known mention in the journals of Tisereldanour of the word indicated it had an anti-magic effect.

rows.source_raw: Reeds - one of eight bands along the Coast. It is a temperate land of lowland and many lakes. Commonly seen as the homeland of humans. Large portions are still cursed, holding entire undead cities, eternally burning valleys, and floating mountains.
rows.context_en: Reeds - one of eight bands along the Coast. It is a temperate land of lowland and many lakes. Commonly seen as the homeland of humans. Large portions are still cursed, holding entire undead cities, eternally burning valleys, and floating mountains.

rows.source_raw: Conchie - slang for conscientious objector. During the Coast War they had a whole penal troop called the Conchie Corp, full of people who were Charmed into performing war-services. Can you imagine? Losing your freedom like that? Awful!
rows.context_en: Conchie - slang for conscientious objector. During the Coast War they had a whole penal troop called the Conchie Corp, full of people who were Charmed into performing war-services. Can you imagine? Losing your freedom like that? Awful!

rows.source_raw: In this, some states hold out better than others. Some floating cities in Old Diminor, specifically that of the Shadow Lord, would grant access to magical food to all the lower classes. Others would offer free education for all children, no matter their birth, in order to find those gifted in the art of the esoteric. Actions like these, along with a steadily rising quality of life, prolonged the spread of working class resentment.
rows.context_en: In this, some states hold out better than others. Some floating cities in Old Diminor, specifically that of the Shadow Lord, would grant access to magical food to all the lower classes. Others would offer free education for all children, no matter their birth, in order to find those gifted in the art of the esoteric. Actions like these, along with a steadily rising quality of life, prolonged the spread of working class resentment.

rows.source_raw: Fire Prevention Declaration - Signed into action a few years ago, the Fire Prevention Declaration is a governmental attempt to curb the spreading of fires by employing always-ready folk in every district. The response times have been pushed down to less than ten minutes.
rows.context_en: Fire Prevention Declaration - Signed into action a few years ago, the Fire Prevention Declaration is a governmental attempt to curb the spreading of fires by employing always-ready folk in every district. The response times have been pushed down to less than ten minutes.

rows.source_raw: Magistrate - five rulers of the five districts. They are the Coinlord, Homelord, Shieldlord, Foodlord, and Lawlord. Each has a unique role in the city's bureaucracy. Sometimes the titles switch between districts, but generally the Housing Districts holds the Homelord, and the Palace holds the Lawlord.
rows.context_en: Magistrate - five rulers of the five districts. They are the Coinlord, Homelord, Shieldlord, Foodlord, and Lawlord. Each has a unique role in the city's bureaucracy. Sometimes the titles switch between districts, but generally the Housing Districts holds the Homelord, and the Palace holds the Lawlord.

rows.source_raw: Sending Pebble - a magic item capable of casting a weak version of the Sending spell, only reaching targets within a kilometer. Due to it not requiring a receiving magic item, unlike a regular Sending Stone, it is exceedingly expensive and rare.
rows.context_en: Sending Pebble - a magic item capable of casting a weak version of the Sending spell, only reaching targets within a kilometer. Due to it not requiring a receiving magic item, unlike a regular Sending Stone, it is exceedingly expensive and rare.

rows.source_raw: Fourth Apprentice of Jor - one of Jor's original apprentices. Referred to as the Nameless Apprentice, due to the original name being cursed. Anyone who heard the name would laugh until they died. Famously imprisoned in Thazen and used as fuel to drive the Haagin-Dim Coalition to near complete domination during the mid Coast War.
rows.context_en: Fourth Apprentice of Jor - one of Jor's original apprentices. Referred to as the Nameless Apprentice, due to the original name being cursed. Anyone who heard the name would laugh until they died. Famously imprisoned in Thazen and used as fuel to drive the Haagin-Dim Coalition to near complete domination during the mid Coast War.

rows.source_raw: With a few quick twirls, you can see how the copper wafers get unaligned with the structure around it. Then another few tugs at the knobs next to it, the angle and probably strength of the entire pressing-mechanism has changed as well.
rows.context_en: With a few quick twirls, you can see how the copper wafers get unaligned with the structure around it. Then another few tugs at the knobs next to it, the angle and probably strength of the entire pressing-mechanism has changed as well.

rows.source_raw: Power Word - a series of powerful spells that focus entirely on enforcing a single reality-shift upon a target. Originally created by dragons, whose magic was verbal only. The early Arcanists learned from their masters and copied many of these words into their own spells.
rows.context_en: Power Word - a series of powerful spells that focus entirely on enforcing a single reality-shift upon a target. Originally created by dragons, whose magic was verbal only. The early Arcanists learned from their masters and copied many of these words into their own spells.

rows.source_raw: Sun - the light of the day. The great source of power that moves alone across the sky over the day. It follows a different pattern of movement in each band, and with different intensities. In Askanii it is small and moves quickly.
rows.context_en: Sun - the light of the day. The great source of power that moves alone across the sky over the day. It follows a different pattern of movement in each band, and with different intensities. In Askanii it is small and moves quickly.

rows.source_raw: Invisible Hand - a term coined by Êirik Smêd, a prominent Arcane Era scholar from Vourgeni. It basically refers to the forces of self-interest and competition that, when operating within a free market, unintentionally guide individual actions toward outcomes that benefit society as a whole. Good shit.
rows.context_en: Invisible Hand - a term coined by Êirik Smêd, a prominent Arcane Era scholar from Vourgeni. It basically refers to the forces of self-interest and competition that, when operating within a free market, unintentionally guide individual actions toward outcomes that benefit society as a whole. Good shit.

rows.source_raw: Coast War - a near century-spanning conflict between every great nation of the Arcane Era. Scholars estimate it claimed more than half of the entire human population. It is said to have begun in 398 AE, by the inciting spark of the Assassination of Priestess Ilona, and it generally agreed to have concluding with the Fall of Thazen in 481 AE, fifty-seven years ago.
rows.context_en: Coast War - a near century-spanning conflict between every great nation of the Arcane Era. Scholars estimate it claimed more than half of the entire human population. It is said to have begun in 398 AE, by the inciting spark of the Assassination of Priestess Ilona, and it generally agreed to have concluding with the Fall of Thazen in 481 AE, fifty-seven years ago.

rows.source_raw: Jor's Point - the legendary first gate. Based on archeological evidence, the Beasts of Jor believes Jor to have first entered this realm at this location, at the farthest point of Jor's Sea, right where all the bands collide. None who have tried sailing to this point have ever returned.
rows.context_en: Jor's Point - the legendary first gate. Based on archeological evidence, the Beasts of Jor believes Jor to have first entered this realm at this location, at the farthest point of Jor's Sea, right where all the bands collide. None who have tried sailing to this point have ever returned.

rows.source_raw: Ventilation System - Tolstad is full of tiny holes and air shafts. These circulate air from the river winds all throughout the tunnels. No one knows fully how they work, but they circulate both heated and cooled air. Most likely magical in nature.
rows.context_en: Ventilation System - Tolstad is full of tiny holes and air shafts. These circulate air from the river winds all throughout the tunnels. No one knows fully how they work, but they circulate both heated and cooled air. Most likely magical in nature.

rows.source_raw: An immigrant of Divinii Torum. An owlfolk. An expert on all things BOOK. He has brought his expertise to your city, and for this you should feel grateful. This is the perfect example of why this new Era is so very, very wonderful.
rows.context_en: An immigrant of Divinii Torum. An owlfolk. An expert on all things BOOK. He has brought his expertise to your city, and for this you should feel grateful. This is the perfect example of why this new Era is so very, very wonderful.

rows.source_raw: Alemikan - one of the last Urthfolk houses to arrive after the founding of Norvik. They originally went east during the Coast War, but a handful of them survived and fled to Norvik with several Arcanist artifacts. Today they are highly revered in the city, the third Lord Patriarch Huggar having been of their bloodline.
rows.context_en: Alemikan - one of the last Urthfolk houses to arrive after the founding of Norvik. They originally went east during the Coast War, but a handful of them survived and fled to Norvik with several Arcanist artifacts. Today they are highly revered in the city, the third Lord Patriarch Huggar having been of their bloodline.

rows.source_raw: Detect Magic - a classic 1st level divination spell. It allows the caster to view magical effect, enchantments, illusions, and other esoterical things clearly within a certain range. You don't have access to this currently. But Detect Secret should be a good substitute.
rows.context_en: Detect Magic - a classic 1st level divination spell. It allows the caster to view magical effect, enchantments, illusions, and other esoterical things clearly within a certain range. You don't have access to this currently. But Detect Secret should be a good substitute.

rows.source_raw: Snail Incident - in 26 PE (last year) an anti-magic snail snuck into the Palace vault's magical barriers and accidentally took Urth's Helm, the most holy artifact in all of Norvik. It was a month long scandal covered by the press in detail as adventurers delved deep underground in search for the snail. However, it turned out the snail never left the vault, and the crown was quickly recovered.
rows.context_en: Snail Incident - in 26 PE (last year) an anti-magic snail snuck into the Palace vault's magical barriers and accidentally took Urth's Helm, the most holy artifact in all of Norvik. It was a month long scandal covered by the press in detail as adventurers delved deep underground in search for the snail. However, it turned out the snail never left the vault, and the crown was quickly recovered.

rows.source_raw: This Illym servant, this spy, this Warden of Outreach - he is but a tendril of his masters. But not so efficient nor successfully scheming as you might imagine. No, they are nowhere near the might of their peak. Illym is dead. This is an avatar of her decaying corpse.
rows.context_en: This Illym servant, this spy, this Warden of Outreach - he is but a tendril of his masters. But not so efficient nor successfully scheming as you might imagine. No, they are nowhere near the might of their peak. Illym is dead. This is an avatar of her decaying corpse.

rows.source_raw: Iomestra - one of Jor's original apprentices. Little is known of her, apart from her symbol (a brachen branch) being littered among certain Gate Era ruins. But your research has revealed it clearly to you: she is a shadow in Askan, choosing to stay hidden. Yet her influence is felt everywhere.
rows.context_en: Iomestra - one of Jor's original apprentices. Little is known of her, apart from her symbol (a brachen branch) being littered among certain Gate Era ruins. But your research has revealed it clearly to you: she is a shadow in Askan, choosing to stay hidden. Yet her influence is felt everywhere.

rows.source_raw: Aevan - one of the many theocratic cities which fell towards the end of the Divine Era. While little information about them survived the Arcane Era, what is remembered in history books is their strict adherence to a set of virtues. Today some scholars view this as a tyranny while others view it as a wisdom as more information is discovered about them.
rows.context_en: Aevan - one of the many theocratic cities which fell towards the end of the Divine Era. While little information about them survived the Arcane Era, what is remembered in history books is their strict adherence to a set of virtues. Today some scholars view this as a tyranny while others view it as a wisdom as more information is discovered about them.

rows.source_raw: Moon - the light of the night. The great source of power that moves alone across the sky at night. It has a different color and follows different movement in each band. In Askanii it is light blue and stays in the sky for far longers than the rest.
rows.context_en: Moon - the light of the night. The great source of power that moves alone across the sky at night. It has a different color and follows different movement in each band. In Askanii it is light blue and stays in the sky for far longers than the rest.

rows.source_raw: Skyline Boys - a magazine written by a group of rogue teenage girls. Mostly contains fan fiction of famous characters from other popular magazines, only here they make them kiss and hold hands and stuff. Usually only male characters, funnily enough.
rows.context_en: Skyline Boys - a magazine written by a group of rogue teenage girls. Mostly contains fan fiction of famous characters from other popular magazines, only here they make them kiss and hold hands and stuff. Usually only male characters, funnily enough.

rows.source_raw: Devil - lawful evil fiend. They are lawyers, soul-traders, and either the most efficient type of leaders or the worst type of middle managers. Their numbers are dwindling as of late, but in Bloodcity Bay they still rule supreme.
rows.context_en: Devil - lawful evil fiend. They are lawyers, soul-traders, and either the most efficient type of leaders or the worst type of middle managers. Their numbers are dwindling as of late, but in Bloodcity Bay they still rule supreme.

rows.source_raw: Ym - one of eight bands along the Coast. Great homeland of the elusive and isolationist high elves. A land of artificial forests, crags covered in eternal darkness, and roots burrowing into the very Coast itself. The Ym have fought a losing battle against devils and celestials for millenia, but their authoritarian society still lives within those great walls.
rows.context_en: Ym - one of eight bands along the Coast. Great homeland of the elusive and isolationist high elves. A land of artificial forests, crags covered in eternal darkness, and roots burrowing into the very Coast itself. The Ym have fought a losing battle against devils and celestials for millenia, but their authoritarian society still lives within those great walls.

rows.source_raw: Wall Enforcer - Urthguard members trained to defend the walls of the city. They typically deal with creatures from the wilds breaching into the districts, even the unofficial ones. Typically some tough bastards, since they get to see so much combat.
rows.context_en: Wall Enforcer - Urthguard members trained to defend the walls of the city. They typically deal with creatures from the wilds breaching into the districts, even the unofficial ones. Typically some tough bastards, since they get to see so much combat.

rows.source_raw: Golden Line - the geographic barrier and magical wall cutting the Clanlands in two. The western side is under occupation of the Golden Horde. This fragile armistice is seen by many as not valid due to the constant guerilla warfare by halflings stuck on the western side.
rows.context_en: Golden Line - the geographic barrier and magical wall cutting the Clanlands in two. The western side is under occupation of the Golden Horde. This fragile armistice is seen by many as not valid due to the constant guerilla warfare by halflings stuck on the western side.

rows.source_raw: Gorgorian - a devil second in line to rule the deepest layer of hell. Legends say he was presented with a deal: Conquer one measly pocket dimension and in exchange he would be granted the throne. A millennia of fighting orcs, elves, several walking gods later, his corpse is now used as the foundation for Bloodcity Bay.
rows.context_en: Gorgorian - a devil second in line to rule the deepest layer of hell. Legends say he was presented with a deal: Conquer one measly pocket dimension and in exchange he would be granted the throne. A millennia of fighting orcs, elves, several walking gods later, his corpse is now used as the foundation for Bloodcity Bay.

rows.source_raw: For example: humans can obviously reproduce with dwarves. But the offspring is always, aesthetically and biologically speaking, very human. Which does not make sense, biologically. We are, scientifically speaking, different species. Dogs do not breed with cats, do they?
rows.context_en: For example: humans can obviously reproduce with dwarves. But the offspring is always, aesthetically and biologically speaking, very human. Which does not make sense, biologically. We are, scientifically speaking, different species. Dogs do not breed with cats, do they?

rows.source_raw: Silverslove - an ancient ruined city, hidden under the marshlands of central Reeds. It was abandoned towards the end of the Draconic Civil War, and very few have managed to ever gain entrance- and ever fewer have ever returned from delving into its trapped and cursed halls.
rows.context_en: Silverslove - an ancient ruined city, hidden under the marshlands of central Reeds. It was abandoned towards the end of the Draconic Civil War, and very few have managed to ever gain entrance- and ever fewer have ever returned from delving into its trapped and cursed halls.

rows.source_raw: He will respect you only if you serve him loyalty, like a hound, or... if you prove yourself competent and ruthless. He has no time for fools. But a man of his stature does not retain power if he does not recognize talent when it barks in his face.
rows.context_en: He will respect you only if you serve him loyalty, like a hound, or... if you prove yourself competent and ruthless. He has no time for fools. But a man of his stature does not retain power if he does not recognize talent when it barks in his face.

rows.source_raw: Fell Tire - one of eight bands along the Coast. A land half-ruined from millenia of warfare, its red lowlands stands for a quarter of the all food production on the Coast, all flowing from the trade ports in Bloodcity Bay. The name 'Fell Tire' either originates in Orcish as 'High Place', or "Bad Fort" in Infernal. Or "Hill Land" in Goblin. It is hotly debated.
rows.context_en: Fell Tire - one of eight bands along the Coast. A land half-ruined from millenia of warfare, its red lowlands stands for a quarter of the all food production on the Coast, all flowing from the trade ports in Bloodcity Bay. The name 'Fell Tire' either originates in Orcish as 'High Place', or "Bad Fort" in Infernal. Or "Hill Land" in Goblin. It is hotly debated.

rows.source_raw: Will Do, General - a funny play. It still runs all the time over on Stage Street, in the Housing District. You saw it once, illegally, by sneaking in during the intermission. The show was... okay. Then again, you only saw half of it, so who knows?
rows.context_en: Will Do, General - a funny play. It still runs all the time over on Stage Street, in the Housing District. You saw it once, illegally, by sneaking in during the intermission. The show was... okay. Then again, you only saw half of it, so who knows?

rows.source_raw: Both Urth and Lord Gorm proved it to me, that some Magistrates of Norvik are actively selling out the city to the Freestriders. This includes casting Feeblemind on the Lord Patriarch some years ago, and keeping it under wraps. Though maybe you're already aware of all this, being a trade prince and all.
rows.context_en: Both Urth and Lord Gorm proved it to me, that some Magistrates of Norvik are actively selling out the city to the Freestriders. This includes casting Feeblemind on the Lord Patriarch some years ago, and keeping it under wraps. Though maybe you're already aware of all this, being a trade prince and all.

rows.source_raw: Born from the Strings of Fates - the Fade Era remnant tyrants smile upon his step. For some very, very, very strange reason. This dwarf, if handed an object with a destiny set in stone, could automatically create <shake>FRAYS</shake>. Chaos. Madness. A new chance to roll the die.
rows.context_en: Born from the Strings of Fates - the Fade Era remnant tyrants smile upon his step. For some very, very, very strange reason. This dwarf, if handed an object with a destiny set in stone, could automatically create <shake>FRAYS</shake>. Chaos. Madness. A new chance to roll the die.

rows.source_raw: Fade Era - a period lasting approximately one thousand one hundred and eleven years, if you trust modern scholars. The second era, also known as the Fall Era, named after the slow decline following Jor shutting his gates. Ended around a thousand years ago.
rows.context_en: Fade Era - a period lasting approximately one thousand one hundred and eleven years, if you trust modern scholars. The second era, also known as the Fall Era, named after the slow decline following Jor shutting his gates. Ended around a thousand years ago.

rows.source_raw: Alignment - an esoteric effect which brands each creature on the Coast with one of nine labels, on two axis: lawful to chaotic, and good to evil. It is debated exactly who or what determines the parameters within this effect. Spells like Detect Alignment are capable of parsing this effect.
rows.context_en: Alignment - an esoteric effect which brands each creature on the Coast with one of nine labels, on two axis: lawful to chaotic, and good to evil. It is debated exactly who or what determines the parameters within this effect. Spells like Detect Alignment are capable of parsing this effect.

rows.source_raw: This woman has found several holes in the city's purse. Over the years she's run factories, founded startups, dealt with city corporations, guild deals, and now she's a tavern proprietor. A highly accomplished woman, the type to flourish in the Freestrider future.
rows.context_en: This woman has found several holes in the city's purse. Over the years she's run factories, founded startups, dealt with city corporations, guild deals, and now she's a tavern proprietor. A highly accomplished woman, the type to flourish in the Freestrider future.

rows.source_raw: Abjuration Lines - the infamous esoteric walls of protective spells kept up by thousands of war wizards and clerics during the early Coast War. They were depreciated due to anti-magic methods during the late stage battles.
rows.context_en: Abjuration Lines - the infamous esoteric walls of protective spells kept up by thousands of war wizards and clerics during the early Coast War. They were depreciated due to anti-magic methods during the late stage battles.

rows.source_raw: Industrial Revolution - a period of rapid technological, esoteric, and social change that began in Old Dominor between 270 and 340 AE. It marked the shift from agrarian, magic-based, and handcraft economies to industrialized and mechanized production. This transformation revolutionized manufacturing, transportation, and urban life, laying the foundation for the modern industrial world.
rows.context_en: Industrial Revolution - a period of rapid technological, esoteric, and social change that began in Old Dominor between 270 and 340 AE. It marked the shift from agrarian, magic-based, and handcraft economies to industrialized and mechanized production. This transformation revolutionized manufacturing, transportation, and urban life, laying the foundation for the modern industrial world.

rows.source_raw: Shifting Falls - a deep cavernous part of the City Below, found underneath the eastern districts of Norvik. It stretches far down all the way to the Gravity Flip, and is the only discovered part of the City Below to do so. Known for its magical waterfalls and hanging architecture.
rows.context_en: Shifting Falls - a deep cavernous part of the City Below, found underneath the eastern districts of Norvik. It stretches far down all the way to the Gravity Flip, and is the only discovered part of the City Below to do so. Known for its magical waterfalls and hanging architecture.

rows.source_raw: There is absolutely an argument there - we Freestriders focus on democracy as a way for folk to free themselves. That influence, as you say, is nothing but an invitation to a broader community of free trade, fortune-building, and most importantly: peace.
rows.context_en: There is absolutely an argument there - we Freestriders focus on democracy as a way for folk to free themselves. That influence, as you say, is nothing but an invitation to a broader community of free trade, fortune-building, and most importantly: peace.

rows.source_raw: Seven Silence - on the seventh Bloodnight you're supposed to stay quiet, and not cause any trouble, as the streets of Norvik are filled with the ghosts of the fallen. Of course, most children love to interact with the harmless ghosts, and usually spend all evening playing games with the spirits.
rows.context_en: Seven Silence - on the seventh Bloodnight you're supposed to stay quiet, and not cause any trouble, as the streets of Norvik are filled with the ghosts of the fallen. Of course, most children love to interact with the harmless ghosts, and usually spend all evening playing games with the spirits.

rows.source_raw: Antesepalian. A creature who was ancient before this world was created. What stands before you is no ambassador- this is a close servant of an outer Power. That is why your body recoils, even when the light appears to signal nothing but BE NOT AFRAID to your senses.
rows.context_en: Antesepalian. A creature who was ancient before this world was created. What stands before you is no ambassador- this is a close servant of an outer Power. That is why your body recoils, even when the light appears to signal nothing but BE NOT AFRAID to your senses.

rows.source_raw: Detect Alignment - a 1st-level divination spell, which allows the caster to see an esoteric effect around creatures who are not true neutral on the alignment axis. Clerics often use this as a utility, even if alignments are not admissible in Norvikian courts since the Urthfolk Rights Order of 499 AE.
rows.context_en: Detect Alignment - a 1st-level divination spell, which allows the caster to see an esoteric effect around creatures who are not true neutral on the alignment axis. Clerics often use this as a utility, even if alignments are not admissible in Norvikian courts since the Urthfolk Rights Order of 499 AE.

rows.source_raw: Walking God - the term used to describe the divine avatars who rose and fell in and around the Divine Era. Most major religions on the Coast today are built around these figures, and their influence can be felt everywhere. Urth was the last known Walking God when he faded, twenty-seven years ago.
rows.context_en: Walking God - the term used to describe the divine avatars who rose and fell in and around the Divine Era. Most major religions on the Coast today are built around these figures, and their influence can be felt everywhere. Urth was the last known Walking God when he faded, twenty-seven years ago.

rows.source_raw: The Reconquest - a historical event in Norvik. It began with Urth's Landing in 466 AE, and is said to have been finished by 468 AE, when Urth finished eradicating the trolls who roamed in the ruins of Toulin'catl. It is a point of contention if it should be called a 'reconquest' or just a 'conquest' as no humans had ever lived in the location before.
rows.context_en: The Reconquest - a historical event in Norvik. It began with Urth's Landing in 466 AE, and is said to have been finished by 468 AE, when Urth finished eradicating the trolls who roamed in the ruins of Toulin'catl. It is a point of contention if it should be called a 'reconquest' or just a 'conquest' as no humans had ever lived in the location before.

rows.source_raw: Positivism - the ideology of scientific truth, that all things, magical or otherwise, can be explained and rationalized. This style of thought was born during the early days of the Arcane Era, to counteract the barbarism of Numenism.
rows.context_en: Positivism - the ideology of scientific truth, that all things, magical or otherwise, can be explained and rationalized. This style of thought was born during the early days of the Arcane Era, to counteract the barbarism of Numenism.

rows.source_raw: Socialism – the softer form of Azgalism. While Azgal envisioned a stateless, classless society, with complete equity for all, socialism is a vision of communal ownership. In practice, this means a mixed economy, where the state acts on behalf of the people and mediates between private initiative and collective needs.
rows.context_en: Socialism – the softer form of Azgalism. While Azgal envisioned a stateless, classless society, with complete equity for all, socialism is a vision of communal ownership. In practice, this means a mixed economy, where the state acts on behalf of the people and mediates between private initiative and collective needs.

rows.source_raw: rights - rights inherent to all sentient beings, regardless of folktype, sex, nationality, ethnicity, language, religion, or any other status. Originally spread by Azgal, and is today a hotly debated topic across the Coast.
rows.context_en: rights - rights inherent to all sentient beings, regardless of folktype, sex, nationality, ethnicity, language, religion, or any other status. Originally spread by Azgal, and is today a hotly debated topic across the Coast.

rows.source_raw: Bloodcity Bay - the most populated city around the Coast. Built upon the corpse of Gorgorian the Great and Terrible. A sprawling mess of wooden walkways and buildings carved out of the calcified remains. Once famous for its fleet of airships deployed during the Coast War. Known as the origin of the Industrial Revolution, and while New Thazen has overtaken it as the leader in manufacturing, it still stands as one of the most industrious city states.
rows.context_en: Bloodcity Bay - the most populated city around the Coast. Built upon the corpse of Gorgorian the Great and Terrible. A sprawling mess of wooden walkways and buildings carved out of the calcified remains. Once famous for its fleet of airships deployed during the Coast War. Known as the origin of the Industrial Revolution, and while New Thazen has overtaken it as the leader in manufacturing, it still stands as one of the most industrious city states.

rows.source_raw: Skaldeman - the first noble house in to be raised by Urth after the founding of Norvik. Famously formed during a party where Johilda, the Bard with the Silver Voice, performed for Urth and he enjoyed it so much that he immediately named her nobility and gave her a piece of the city to watch over. Her descendants became loyal servants to this day.
rows.context_en: Skaldeman - the first noble house in to be raised by Urth after the founding of Norvik. Famously formed during a party where Johilda, the Bard with the Silver Voice, performed for Urth and he enjoyed it so much that he immediately named her nobility and gave her a piece of the city to watch over. Her descendants became loyal servants to this day.

rows.source_raw: The city is changing. As are you. You have always been volatile in your political views, but now it's time to choose, for real. You have unlocked the ability to ask people what they are voting for. A deep urge inside of you is calling: you need to ask. You MUST ask. Over and over again. At least twenty times. Okay, ten. You have currently asked people $DEM times.

You have unlocked the ability to ask people what they are voting for. A deep urge inside of you is calling: you need to ask. You MUST ask. Over and over again. At least twenty times. Okay, ten. You have currently asked people $DEM times.

rows.source_raw: As with all things, it is ECONOMY which steers the masses. The flow of CAPITAL, as you have read so much about. These boys might rage against the world if they wish, yet if they each held a role in society which paid them well and granted them satisfaction, then all that rage would subside. They simply require a new ideology. One which supports their economic interests, and nothing else.

They simply require a new ideology. One which supports their economic interests, and nothing else.

rows.source_raw: Azgal Ashmask - a dwarf of a low caste born deep in the Lekm-Muraz tunnels. When he attained divinity in 443 AE, he crushed the kingdoms and formed the Azgal Folkhem Forge. On his death in 470 AE, a great civil war broke out, resulting in the exiling of all his followers to the surface. But his influence was felt all around the Undercoast and above, as his Exiled dwarves spread Azgalism to all corners of Jor.

rows.source_raw: Brian Prax - known as the most incompetent Arcanist to have ever studied the Strings. Ruled House Prax for six days before he managed to anger a local lich by being unable to pronounce his name correctly in a public address. He was Power Word Killed right on stage. People cheered when he died, if the story is to be believed.

People cheered when he died, if the story is to be believed.

rows.source_raw: This one has lived a wild life. You can see it in her eyes- the years spent in service. The dream of a greater life. The bodies left in her wake. This hagling has nothing but a desperate love for life and freedom at this point. Let her have it.

rows.source_raw: Great Migration - the day the Urthfolk arrived on the Askanii shores for the first time. Often celebrated by cleaning and preparing boats for the first breaking of the ice. But since it's no longer as cold as it used to be in March, people often head out on fishing trips to celebrate.
rows.context_en: Great Migration - the day the Urthfolk arrived on the Askanii shores for the first time. Often celebrated by cleaning and preparing boats for the first breaking of the ice. But since it's no longer as cold as it used to be in March, people often head out on fishing trips to celebrate.

rows.source_raw: Holswyth - a city founded within the confines of a massive Gate Era castle, leaning out over the Coast. Known for the Holswyth Ting, and is essentially just a city built out of a series of complex embassies and gnomish industries.
rows.context_en: Holswyth - a city founded within the confines of a massive Gate Era castle, leaning out over the Coast. Known for the Holswyth Ting, and is essentially just a city built out of a series of complex embassies and gnomish industries.

rows.source_raw: Loxorii - elephant folk. Known for the massive size, and ability to keep growing their entire lives. As part-celestials, most of them live in Divinii Torum and Ym. Although some are known to migrate peacefully into the valleys of Vyz-Kha Peaks from their Girikut homeland. The dwarves generally have a very good relationship with them.

Although some are known to migrate peacefully into the valleys of Vyz-Kha Peaks from their Girikut homeland. The dwarves generally have a very good relationship with them.

rows.source_raw: The Housing District - one of five districts in Norvik. The poorer area on the west side of the river. Named as such due to the Urthfolk originally being housed here while Urth cleared out the ruins in what is now known as The Palace.
rows.context_en: The Housing District - one of five districts in Norvik. The poorer area on the west side of the river. Named as such due to the Urthfolk originally being housed here while Urth cleared out the ruins in what is now known as The Palace.

rows.source_raw: Iron Door - the name of a legendary artifact crafted by Gate Era artificers. They are doors leading to nowhere, instead capturing any creature in a pocket dimension. They were used by the Beasts of Jor as cells for their most powerful prisoners.
rows.context_en: Iron Door - the name of a legendary artifact crafted by Gate Era artificers. They are doors leading to nowhere, instead capturing any creature in a pocket dimension. They were used by the Beasts of Jor as cells for their most powerful prisoners.

rows.source_raw: Coven - a very large coven, run like a corporation. Usually working alongside their chosen city states. There are four of them on the Coast. Officially. The biggest is the Hexbrew Inkorporation, working directly with the Freestriders.
rows.context_en: Coven - a very large coven, run like a corporation. Usually working alongside their chosen city states. There are four of them on the Coast. Officially. The biggest is the Hexbrew Inkorporation, working directly with the Freestriders.

rows.source_raw: Muletown Incident - in 25 PE (two years ago) a reported fight with a local Arcanist occured in the northernmost Norvikian settlement of Urthport. Ever since the city has been overrun with mules, which the locals are unable to get rid off. Thus the settlement is commonly known as Muletown.
rows.context_en: Muletown Incident - in 25 PE (two years ago) a reported fight with a local Arcanist occured in the northernmost Norvikian settlement of Urthport. Ever since the city has been overrun with mules, which the locals are unable to get rid off. Thus the settlement is commonly known as Muletown.

rows.source_raw: Dim Coalition - the united magocracy of the Hagginshags and Diminorii. This was arguably the most powerful of the Arcanist-states, said to rival the strength of warfare during the Gate Era conflicts. Their merger is considered one of the first bricks laid on the road to the Coast War.
rows.context_en: Dim Coalition - the united magocracy of the Hagginshags and Diminorii. This was arguably the most powerful of the Arcanist-states, said to rival the strength of warfare during the Gate Era conflicts. Their merger is considered one of the first bricks laid on the road to the Coast War.

rows.source_raw: Feeblemind - an 8th level Enchantment spell. It deals a destructive psychic blow to the target's mind, leaving them in a demented state. In good cases, it can be reversed with a Greater Restoration spell. In bad cases, only a Wish spell can do anything to bring back the target from the abyss.
rows.context_en: Feeblemind - an 8th level Enchantment spell. It deals a destructive psychic blow to the target's mind, leaving them in a demented state. In good cases, it can be reversed with a Greater Restoration spell. In bad cases, only a Wish spell can do anything to bring back the target from the abyss.

rows.source_raw: Era - modern scholars divide history on JOR into five eras. They use canonically important events as starting points for these eras, although this was easier when the Arcanists of Old Diminor still had a common consensus. Not everyone agrees the so-called 'Postwar Era' has even begun, let alone what it should be called.

Not everyone agrees the so-called 'Postwar Era' has even begun, let alone what it should be called.

rows.source_raw: As Snurre attempted to copy the foreword, the esoterically summoned spirit of Cividix himself became near corporeal, possessing Snurre in the process. He got exorcised in the end, and managed to both copy the foreword and gain great insight into the secrets of Cividix.
rows.context_en: As Snurre attempted to copy the foreword, the esoterically summoned spirit of Cividix himself became near corporeal, possessing Snurre in the process. He got exorcised in the end, and managed to both copy the foreword and gain great insight into the secrets of Cividix.

rows.source_raw: Restoration - a series of spells which cure various ailments. The most common are Lesser Restoration and Greater Restoration. They cure anything from blindness to tummy aches. Through their practical implementation from churches across the Coast, most common health issues are eradicated.
rows.context_en: Restoration - a series of spells which cure various ailments. The most common are Lesser Restoration and Greater Restoration. They cure anything from blindness to tummy aches. Through their practical implementation from churches across the Coast, most common health issues are eradicated.

rows.source_raw: Ragn Hemlin. Our speaking came at an inopportune moment. I wish to proclaim an additional thing to you: I found your behavior reprehensible and crude. You represent exactly what we in the Golden Horde believe to be the nature of humanity. You are clearly less special than I imagined, and I apologize for attempting to communicate.
rows.context_en: Ragn Hemlin. Our speaking came at an inopportune moment. I wish to proclaim an additional thing to you: I found your behavior reprehensible and crude. You represent exactly what we in the Golden Horde believe to be the nature of humanity. You are clearly less special than I imagined, and I apologize for attempting to communicate.

rows.source_raw: Universal suffrage. Sure. But nine out of ten guild leaders and business owners are human men." She taps her fingers to list off her talking points. "Women earn lower salaries across all work sectors. Citizenship is withheld from Othered folktypes, on the basis of so-called 'monstrous' traits. Halflings are constantly extradited to the Horde, while every major faction ignores their pleas. Mind-control being used - somehow - as a legal tool of the state!

rows.source_raw: Regenerate - a seventh level transmutation spell. One of the few higher tier magics still commonly in use by clerics and druids across the Coast. It heals and causes lost limbs to essentially regrow or reattach. Various versions of the spell can be required, depending on the affliction.
rows.context_en: Regenerate - a seventh level transmutation spell. One of the few higher tier magics still commonly in use by clerics and druids across the Coast. It heals and causes lost limbs to essentially regrow or reattach. Various versions of the spell can be required, depending on the affliction.

rows.source_raw: Gate Era - a period lasting approximately three thousand years. The first era, named after the many gates opened by Jor to connect his realm with all others. Ended around two thousand years ago, if you trust modern scholars.
rows.context_en: Gate Era - a period lasting approximately three thousand years. The first era, named after the many gates opened by Jor to connect his realm with all others. Ended around two thousand years ago, if you trust modern scholars.

rows.source_raw: Azgalist - Whenever you take damage, move one step forward on a secret three step counter. On step one, a 'thesis' d20 die is thrown. On step two, if the damage you took is higher than the 'thesis' die, an 'antithesis' d4 die is thrown. On step three, the damage you took is lowered either by 1, or by the 'antithesis' die if that die was rolled during step two. This is 'synthesis', somehow. The next time you take damage, return to step one.

On step two, if the damage you took is higher than the 'thesis' die, an 'antithesis' d4 die is thrown. On step three, the damage you took is lowered either by 1, or by the 'antithesis' die if that die was rolled during step two. This is 'synthesis', somehow. The next time you take damage, return to step one.

rows.source_raw: Pest Control Guild - one of the oldest guilds in Norvik. They are supposed to keep monsters at bay, and clean up any unwanted critters across the city. These days, they are so bogged down in requests from citizens and the city that they usually have backlogs for months.
rows.context_en: Pest Control Guild - one of the oldest guilds in Norvik. They are supposed to keep monsters at bay, and clean up any unwanted critters across the city. These days, they are so bogged down in requests from citizens and the city that they usually have backlogs for months.

rows.source_raw: Spoken Word - a second level transmutation spell. It allows the caster to engrave a spoken message onto a surface. Whoever touches it will telepathically hear the message in their mind. However, this spell has been mostly used for the last two centuries as a way of mass-producing musical records.
rows.context_en: Spoken Word - a second level transmutation spell. It allows the caster to engrave a spoken message onto a surface. Whoever touches it will telepathically hear the message in their mind. However, this spell has been mostly used for the last two centuries as a way of mass-producing musical records.

rows.source_raw: Outer God - the term used to describe the actual gods, those who invaded The Coast during the Gate War. They hold no dominion on Jor's Realm since the gates closed, but the most common consensus among scholars is: all walking gods are simply avatars of these real, outer ones.
rows.context_en: Outer God - the term used to describe the actual gods, those who invaded The Coast during the Gate War. They hold no dominion on Jor's Realm since the gates closed, but the most common consensus among scholars is: all walking gods are simply avatars of these real, outer ones.

rows.source_raw: This is what their movement needs. All movements. A shy and terrified witness who will, at each turn, ask everyone around her to rethink their rash decisions. Extremely annoying. But nonetheless... very, very good for society.
rows.context_en: This is what their movement needs. All movements. A shy and terrified witness who will, at each turn, ask everyone around her to rethink their rash decisions. Extremely annoying. But nonetheless... very, very good for society.

rows.source_raw: Lesser Restoration - a second level Abjuration spell. It removes one affliction upon a target. Depending on the severity of it, the spell can remove various birth defects, cripplings, blindness, deafness, as well as minor diseases. Any place of healing across the Coast employs the use of this spell.

Any place of healing across the Coast employs the use of this spell.

rows.source_raw: Umbrameexus - the All-Wise one. The black dragon of Old Diminor, known for holding the largest dragondom at his peak. His capital was the underground city of Silverslove, constructed under the central marshland of the band. His strength was based on esoteric might, and he was known to carry a hundred human wizards on his back when he went to war.

His capital was the underground city of Silverslove, constructed under the central marshland of the band. His strength was based on esoteric might, and he was known to carry a hundred human wizards on his back when he went to war.

rows.source_raw: Middle Edge - a point deep within Jor's Ocean, around the gravity flip, where aquatic folk have built cities within stable water bubbles. A handful of pipe-mountains pierce these bubbles, allowing dry connections with the Undersea and Great Ocean islands. Populated mainly by Anura and Merfolk.
rows.context_en: Middle Edge - a point deep within Jor's Ocean, around the gravity flip, where aquatic folk have built cities within stable water bubbles. A handful of pipe-mountains pierce these bubbles, allowing dry connections with the Undersea and Great Ocean islands. Populated mainly by Anura and Merfolk.

rows.source_raw: Cowboy Rebellion - a minor footnote in the early Coast War, when a union of cow herders in Old Diminor revolted against one of the first Magocrats. Famously, they were known to make use of intelligent horses for their guerilla tactics, including using entire battalions of horses equipped with portable rifles.

Famously, they were known to make use of intelligent horses for their guerilla tactics, including using entire battalions of horses equipped with portable rifles.

rows.source_raw: Top Watcher - high ranking Urthguard member working as a leader of a unit of Street Watchers. Many of them are low-ranking clerics, in order to leverage magic in their criminal investigations. This is the rank you aimed for, before getting scoped up into Tower politics.
